title: The quietest place I know
summary: Describe the most peaceful place you know and why it feels quiet.
prompt: Write 150–250 words about the quietest place you know. Describe what is around you there, what sounds you can or cannot hear, and why you go there. Use present simple and the superlative 'the quietest'.
hintStarters: The quietest place I know is…
When I sit there, I can hear…
sampleText: The quietest place I know is a small library in Helsinki. It is not the biggest library in the city, and it is not the most famous one. But for me, it is the most peaceful. The building is old, with a wooden floor and tall windows. The light is soft, even on a cloudy day. When I open the door, the noise of the street stops, like someone closed a heavy curtain behind me. I always sit at the same table in the corner. The table is by a tall window, and I can see a thin tree outside. When I sit there, I can hear three things only: the slow sound of a page turning, the careful footsteps of another reader, and my own breathing. Nobody talks. Nobody uses a phone. The air smells a little of old books and clean wood. I go there when my head feels too full of voices. After thirty minutes, my thoughts move slower. I can finally think clearly about my work, my family, or a small problem that felt big before. I am not religious, but for me this library is something close to a quiet church. It is the only place in the city where I am completely sure that nobody will ask me a question.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Superlative form
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'The quietest place', 'the biggest library', 'the most famous one' — short adjectives thêm '-est', adjectives dài dùng 'most'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The quietest place', 'the biggest library', 'the most famous one'. Short adjectives add '-est'; longer adjectives use 'most'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Time linker 'when'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I go there when my head feels too full of voices.' — 'When' giới thiệu điều kiện thời gian.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I go there when my head feels too full of voices.' 'When' introduces a time condition.

sampleTranslationVi: Nơi yên tĩnh nhất tôi biết là một thư viện nhỏ ở Helsinki. Nó không phải thư viện lớn nhất thành phố, và cũng không phải nổi tiếng nhất. Nhưng với tôi, đó là nơi bình yên nhất. Tòa nhà cũ, có sàn gỗ và những cửa sổ cao. Ánh sáng dịu, ngay cả trong ngày nhiều mây. Khi tôi mở cửa, tiếng ồn ngoài đường ngừng lại, như có ai vừa kéo một tấm rèm dày sau lưng tôi. Tôi luôn ngồi ở cùng một chiếc bàn trong góc. Chiếc bàn cạnh cửa sổ cao, và tôi có thể nhìn thấy một cái cây gầy bên ngoài. Khi tôi ngồi đó, tôi chỉ nghe ba thứ: tiếng lật trang chậm rãi, bước chân cẩn thận của người đọc khác, và hơi thở của chính mình. Không ai nói chuyện. Không ai dùng điện thoại. Không khí thoảng mùi sách cũ và gỗ sạch. Tôi đến đó khi đầu mình quá nhiều giọng nói. Sau ba mươi phút, ý nghĩ của tôi chậm lại. Tôi mới có thể suy nghĩ rõ ràng về công việc, gia đình, hoặc một vấn đề nhỏ trước đó tưởng lớn. Tôi không theo tôn giáo nào, nhưng với tôi thư viện này gần giống một nhà thờ yên tĩnh. Đây là nơi duy nhất trong thành phố mà tôi chắc chắn không ai sẽ hỏi tôi câu nào.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the superlative correctly?
options: The quietest place I know is a small library in Helsinki.
The most quietest place I know is a small library in Helsinki.
The quiet place I know most is a small library in Helsinki.
The more quiet place I know is a small library in Helsinki.
It is not the biggest library in the city.
It is not the most biggest library in the city.
It is not biggest library in the city.
It is not the bigger library in the city.
The building is old, with a wooden floor and tall windows.
The building old is, with wooden floor and windows tall.
The building is old, with wooden floor and the tall windows.
The building is olds, with a wood floor and talls window.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adjectives are uninflected; word order follows standard English.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'when' correctly to give a time condition?
options: I go there when my head feels too full of voices.
I go there when my head feeling too full of voices.
I going there when my head feels too full of voices.
I go there when my head will feel too full of voices.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses are present simple, which fits a habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Habits → present simple in both clauses.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses the correct preposition?
options: The table is by a tall window.
The table is on a tall window.
The table is in a tall window.
The table is over a tall window.
The air smells a little of old books.
The air smells a little from old books.
The air is smelling little of old books.
The air smell a little to old books.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smell of + noun' is the standard pattern; 'a little' is the adverb of degree.

options: After thirty minutes, my thoughts move slower.
After thirty minutes, my thoughts moves slower.
After thirty minutes, my thoughts more slowly move.
After thirty minutes, my thought moving more slow.
mcQuestions.explanation: Plural subject 'thoughts' takes 'move'; 'slower' is informal but acceptable at A2.
mcQuestions.hint: Subject-verb agreement: plural subject + base verb.

options: I am not religious, but for me this library is something close to a quiet church.
I am not religious, so for me this library is something close to a quiet church.
I am not religious, because for me this library is something close to a quiet church.
I am not religious, while for me this library is something close to a quiet church.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows the contrast between not being religious and feeling something spiritual.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about sound.
options: I can hear three things only: page turning, footsteps, and breathing.
I can hearing three things only: page turning, footsteps, and breathing.
I can hear three thing only: page turn, footstep, and breath.
I hear can three things only: page turning, footsteps and breathing.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Can + base verb' and plural -ing nouns are correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which closing line feels personal and concrete?
options: It is the only place where I am completely sure that nobody will ask me a question.
In conclusion, silence is golden for everyone.
Throughout history, libraries have offered peace.
It is important to note that quiet places benefit mental health.
mcQuestions.explanation: A specific personal observation beats generic AI phrasing.
mcQuestions.hint: Avoid AI clichés like 'In conclusion' or 'It is important to note'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Do you need a quiet place to think clearly, or can you think clearly anywhere? What does your mind need in order to slow down?
